svg.text: USB XInput gamepad → BL616 host → RP2040-Zero → AD5263 digipot / AHCT125 → Apple II game port
1. Power domains: U2/U3 run from the Apple's game-port +5V (verify against AD5263 min single-supply, ≥4.5V). U1 is powered by USB VBUS only; do NOT
join the Zero's 5V pin to +5V_A2 (would back-feed the BL616 hub). Grounds are commoned at one point.
2. Paddle channels are rheostat-mode: A=+5V, wiper (+B tied) toward PDL pin. Firmware enforces wiper code ≥2 (≈1.6kΩ) to cap wiper current during the
558 trigger/recharge; power-on preset is midscale = centered stick. R1-R4 add transient margin; offset ≈ 0.2 PDL count.
3. Buttons are ACTIVE-HIGH. U3 drives +5V only while pressed (OE# low) and is hi-Z when released, so it never fights the IIe/IIgs keyboard pull-downs
on SW0/SW1 (Open/Closed-Apple). R9-R11 emulate the pull-downs a real controller carries — required on II/II+ where open SW inputs float high.
4. AD5263 SDO is open-drain referenced to VDD=5V — leave open; never route to the RP2040 (not 5V tolerant) without a 3.3V-only pull-up.
5. DIS pin selects SPI vs I²C — strap polarity and ALL U2 pin numbers must be verified against the ADI datasheet before layout (unreachable from the
design sandbox; symbol uses logical names). AD0/AD1 are I²C address inputs — tie to GND for SPI use.
6. AD5263 digital inputs are TTL-threshold (VIH 2.4V @ VDD=5V) with internal level shift — 3.3V SPI from U1 is in spec. Same for U3 (AHCT inputs).
7. J1 (16-pin DIP) and J2 (DB9M) are wired in parallel; populate one per build. IIe rear DB9 carries only PDL0/1 + SW0/1; full 4-paddle/3-button
needs J1 on the motherboard socket (II/II+/IIe) or a IIgs (either connector). J1 pin 5 ($C040 strobe) and AN0-AN3 are intentionally unused.
8. 10k pull-ups to 3V3 hold CS#/OE# inactive while the RP2040 boots. Digipot end-to-end tolerance is ±20-30%: keep a per-axis scale factor in settings.
